item.title: From Bestseller to Classic: What Makes a Book Endure?
item.name: From Bestseller to Classic: What Makes a Book Endure?
item.summary: Why do some books become classics? Two book buffs join us to explore why certain novels stand the test of time. Elizabeth Rosner is joined in lively conversation with veteran critic John McMurtrie (formerly of the San Francisco Chronicle ) about what makes certain novels resonate with readers over time.

copy.x: Tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto: why do some books become classics while others fade? Elizabeth Rosner and critic John McMurtrie dig into that question at 6 PM. Worth the drive. https://www.keplers.org/upcoming-events-internal/classic-books
copy.threads: Tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto, two people who've spent their lives thinking about books sit down to ask the big question: why do some novels last forever while others disappear? Elizabeth Rosner and veteran critic John McMurtrie (formerly of the SF Chronicle) get into it at 6 PM. Free, smart, and the kind of conversation you'll keep having on the drive home. 📚 https://www.keplers.org/upcoming-events-internal/classic-books #PaloAlto #BookLovers #SouthBay
copy.bluesky: Tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto — Elizabeth Rosner and SF Chronicle critic John McMurtrie tackle the question: what actually makes a book a classic? 6 PM, and it's the kind of talk that'll rearrange your reading list. #BookLovers #PaloAlto
copy.facebook: If you love books and have ever wondered why certain novels stick around for centuries while others vanish, tonight at Kepler's in Palo Alto is worth your evening. Author Elizabeth Rosner sits down with veteran critic John McMurtrie — formerly of the San Francisco Chronicle — to dig into exactly that question. It starts at 6 PM and sounds like the kind of conversation that's genuinely hard to leave. https://www.keplers.org/upcoming-events-internal/classic-books
copy.instagram:
Tonight's pick for the South Bay 📚

Kepler's Books in Palo Alto is hosting one of those conversations that sounds simple on the surface — why do some books become classics? — but gets surprisingly deep once you're in the room.

Author Elizabeth Rosner is joined by John McMurtrie, a veteran literary critic who spent years at the San Francisco Chronicle, and together they're going to pull apart what makes certain novels resonate with readers across generations while others quietly disappear.

It starts at 6 PM. If you've ever finished a book and thought "this one's going to matter for a long time" — or picked up a so-called classic and wondered why anyone bothered — this is your crowd.

Kepler's has been running conversations like this for decades, and they tend to feel more like a great dinner party discussion than a lecture. Come with opinions.

cards.why: Affordable, unpretentious, and the kind of place that just hits right after a morning outside.

cards.name: Spring Wine Walk
cards.blurb: Head over to Los Gatos for the Spring Wine Walk. You stroll through downtown, pop into shops and tasting spots, and sip your way through the afternoon.

cards.name: OMESCAPE | Sunnyvale
cards.address: 1135 E Arques Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Book a room at OMESCAPE and work through one of their escape room challenges with your crew — they're genuinely well-designed and the rating speaks for itself.
cards.why: Perfect indoor activity on a mellow Sunday morning before things get crowded.

cards.name: Opelia
cards.address: 25 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Opelia for lunch — it's a solid sit-down spot in downtown Los Gatos with a tight window, so go early and settle in on the patio if they've got space.
cards.why: Los Gatos on a sunny Sunday is exactly where you want to be for a leisurely lunch.

cards.name: St. Joseph's Hill Open Space Preserve
cards.address: Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Burn off lunch on the trails at St. Joseph's Hill Open Space Preserve — the views over the Los Gatos hills are worth every step and the upper loop gives you a real sense of the landscape.
cards.why: 75 degrees and sunny with trails right in Los Gatos is a hard combo to beat.

cards.name: Heroes & Champions
cards.address: 574 E El Camino Real, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Swing into Heroes & Champions in Sunnyvale and dig through the comics, collectibles, and gaming gear — it's the kind of shop you don't rush through.
cards.why: Great way to wind down the afternoon before dinner without committing to anything too serious.

cards.name: Salt & Straw
cards.address: 2855 Stevens Creek Blvd, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Salt & Straw for a scoop or two before dinner — their rotating seasonal flavors are always interesting and the lines move fast on a Sunday evening.
cards.why: It's a genuinely good ice cream break and the Stevens Creek location is easy to pop into.

cards.name: Fox Hole Golf Club
cards.address: 545 Aldo Ave #13, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the night with a round at Fox Hole Golf Club, a bar and mini golf setup in Santa Clara that's perfect for a Sunday evening with drinks in hand.
cards.why: It's fun, it's low-stakes, and it's a much better Sunday night than sitting on a couch.

cards.blurb: Walk or bike the trails around Shoreline Lake with the Bay stretching out in front of you. Cool morning air, wide open views, and almost no one out on a Monday.

copy.instagram:
Tonight's pick 🎶

"Poems and Dances from the East" is at @mountainviewcenter tonight at 7:30 PM, and it sounds like something genuinely worth showing up for.

The Stanford Baipu Chinese Music Group is performing world premiere works by composer Leonid Iogansen alongside curated pieces for Chinese instruments — all of it built around this beautiful tension between Eastern and Western musical traditions. Not a concept album. A live concert, in a room, tonight.

If you've never heard Chinese instruments like the erhu or pipa in a concert hall setting, this is a good reason to find out what you've been missing. 🪗

item.title: Draft Active Transportation Plan Public Workshop
item.summary: Community workshop to review the public draft of the Mountain View Active Transportation Plan and share input on creating a safer, more connected network for walking, biking, and rolling.

copy.x: Tonight in Mountain View: the city wants your input on how people walk, bike, and roll around town. 6 PM at City Hall. If you've ever wished a crosswalk existed or a bike lane connected somewhere useful — this is the room to say it. https://collaborate.mountainview.gov/atp
copy.threads: Tonight in Mountain View, the city is holding a public workshop on its Active Transportation Plan — basically, how do we make walking, biking, and rolling around town safer and more connected? 6 PM at City Hall. If you have thoughts on a missing sidewalk, a sketchy intersection, or a bike route that goes nowhere useful, this is your moment. https://collaborate.mountainview.gov/atp #MountainView #ActiveTransportation #SouthBay
copy.bluesky: Tonight at 6 PM, Mountain View wants your input on walking, biking, and rolling around the city. Got a crosswalk that should exist or a bike lane that goes nowhere? City Hall is the place to say it. 🚲 https://collaborate.mountainview.gov/atp #MountainView #SouthBay
copy.facebook: Tonight in Mountain View — if you've ever thought "this intersection is dangerous" or "why is there no bike lane here," the city is literally asking for your opinion. The Active Transportation Plan public workshop is at City Hall starting at 6 PM. They're reviewing a draft plan for safer, better-connected walking, biking, and rolling routes, and community input shapes what actually gets built. Worth showing up. https://collaborate.mountainview.gov/atp
copy.instagram:
Tonight in Mountain View, the city is opening up its Active Transportation Plan for public input — and they genuinely want to hear from you. 🚶🚲

The workshop kicks off at 6 PM at Mountain View City Hall. The plan focuses on making it safer and easier to get around by foot, bike, or anything in between. If you've ever dodged a car in a crosswalk that felt too short, or wished a bike lane actually connected to something useful, this is where that frustration turns into something constructive.

Public workshops like this are how neighborhoods actually change. Show up, look at the draft plan, and tell them what's missing or what's working.

Details and more info at the link below 👇
https://collaborate.mountainview.gov/atp

cards.name: Daily Donuts & Sandwiches
cards.address: 708 Fair Oaks Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a donut and coffee to kick things off — classic old-school donut shop energy with a solid lineup of fresh-made options.
cards.why: Perfect low-key start before the day gets going.

cards.name: Picchetti Winery
cards.address: 13100 Montebello Rd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Head up into the Cupertino hills to Picchetti Winery for a tasting on the patio, surrounded by old-growth vines and orchard views.
cards.why: A 62-degree afternoon at a hilltop winery with a 4.6 reputation is hard to argue with.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at Milpitas Library
cards.blurb: Practice speaking English with peers, pick up conversational skills, and learn about different cultures.
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Sandbaggers Golf Spot
cards.address: 2292 Quimby Rd, San Jose, CA 95122, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a club and work on your short game at this laid-back golf spot — casual enough for a weeknight warm-up without the full-course commitment.
cards.why: It's a fun, low-key way to wind down a Wednesday evening with some friendly competition.

cards.name: Mountain View Sports Pavilion
cards.address: 1185 Castro St, Mountain View, CA 94040, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the night with an evening session at this local sports pavilion — great for a casual game or just soaking up the active atmosphere.
cards.why: It keeps the energy going after dinner without straying far from the South Bay core.
